title: FST Parameter Dumping Stopped Working (in a certain situation?)
body:
Hi there,

I'm experiencing a strange problem.  Recently, parameters dumped via --trace-params all show up as XXX in GTKWave when dumping in the FST format. Real parameters show up as nan.

Parameter values get used properly in the Verilog code (assignments, widths, etc.).  The issue doesn't affect VCD dumping.  I've determined that it's not something that was introduced to Verilator recently (I've tried from 4.026 back to 4.014).  It affecs all parameters regardless of where they are declared in the code.  

If I include my header file, which contains all of the parameters, in a simple project, the parameters all show up correctly in the FST file.  This isn't a real apples to apples test though, as it's compiled under Linux to a Linux executable.  In my real application, the Verilated output is compiled by Ming32 (32-bit) into a Windows DLL and run under Wine.  I could probably make a better apples to apples test, but it's a non-trivial task, and I'm hoping that someone familiar with Verilator internals might be able to point me in the right direction.

I've Verilated with '--debug --stats-vars' and can't see anything obviously amiss.  The project Verilates and compiles cleanly, with no warnings.

Everything otherwise simulates and displays in the FST file as expected.  So whatever the issue is, it just seems to affect parameter dumping in FST (maybe in a Ming32-generated DLL and maybe under Wine).  It worked fine until recently. 

Any idea how I might determine the cause of this issue?  I supposed it could've started with a Wine update (DLL is run under Wine), or even a recent Ming32 update.

Thanks,
Richard
